title: rubus × excultiformis
intro: Rubus × excultiformis, also known as the cultivated raspberry hybrid, is a cross between a black raspberry (Rubus occidentalis) and a red raspberry (Rubus idaeus), resulting in a unique plant with desirable characteristics. The leaves and roots of Rubus × excultiformis have been used in traditional medicine for their antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which make them effective in treating various health conditions. The plant is rich in vitamins, minerals, and dietary fiber, providing numerous health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting digestive health. Rubus × excultiformis can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or dried extract, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote overall well-being and support the immune system.
